topic: The Monitoring Illusion — post-EXP-089 restructure review
change_summary: EXP-089 integrated: body paragraph restructured to lead with none/unmonitored finding (58.6%); ISS-830 also addressed (misleading count). Reviewing whether restructure was correctly executed and whether previously flagged factual errors in the stratification paragraph were fixed.
our_argument_summary: Section 2b.1 argues the dome's monitoring dashboard is an illusion. The EXP-089 restructure correctly reorders the 'What the code actually does' paragraph to lead with the most shocking finding: {{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs (58.6%) have no validation code at all. The hardcoded finding follows second. The core restructure improves the argument's impact. However, the stratification paragraph and contrast paragraph still contain multiple factual errors from pre-restructure text that were NOT fixed by EXP-089.
improvements_since_cycle_2: Body paragraph now leads with none/unmonitored finding (58.6%) — correctly implements EXP-089's primary goal
Bolded emphasis on the none count: '{{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs have no validation code at all' — good visual hierarchy
TLDR 50% error floor correctly scoped to 'NMP Drift Rate domain' (c2 major hole fixed)
Misleading count updated from 'Twelve' to 'Fifteen' (matches current data at 15)

holes_found.description: TLDR-body structural mismatch. The body was restructured (per EXP-089) to lead with the 'none'/unmonitored finding as the primary shock. But the TLDR still leads with hardcoded: '{{CA_HARDCODED}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} monitored domains compare static predicted values against static observed values.' The TLDR doesn't mention unmonitored WINs at all — the word 'unmonitored' or 'none' never appears. A reader who sees only the TLDR takes away 'hardcoded checks are the problem.' A reader who expands gets 'over half have NO checks at all.' These are different messages. Per project rules, a TLDR that misrepresents the body's primary finding is major.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite TLDR to lead with the none finding: 'Direct examination of monitor.py reveals that {{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs have no validation code at all — over half the dome's "confirmed predictions" are completely unmonitored. Of the remainder, {{CA_HARDCODED}} use hardcoded validation where pred==obs, and only {{CA_LIVE}} fetch live data — all from globe-calibrated sources.'

holes_found.description: WIN-068 mislabeled as self-contradicted. The stratification paragraph says 'Eight are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-044/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity, WIN-068 tidal model).' But wins.json shows WIN-068 verdict = 'Std Model Explains', NOT 'Self-Contradicted'. WIN-068 belongs in the standard-physics category, not self-contradicted. This error was NOT flagged in c2 (c2 flagged WIN-045/046/049 as wrongly included because they are hardcoded, not because of verdict — a different error that was fixed). The current error is that WIN-068 is genuinely unmonitored (monitoring=none, correct) but is NOT self-contradicted (verdict=Std Model Explains). A dome defender who checks WIN-068's page on our own site will find it listed under 'Std Model Explains', contradicting this paragraph.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove WIN-068 from the self-contradicted list. Correct count is 7 self-contradicted WINs with monitoring=none: WIN-044, 050, 051, 053, 056, 061, 067. Add WIN-068 to the standard-physics count, making it 12.

holes_found.description: Self-contradicted total count wrong. The contrast paragraph states 'Of the dome's 16 self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), eight have no monitoring at all — but the other eight (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass.' The actual data: 14 WINs have verdict=Self-Contradicted (not 16). Of these, 7 have monitoring=none and 7 have monitoring=hardcoded. The '16' and 'eight/eight' split are both wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: Correct to: 'Of the dome's {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradicted WINs (Part 2), 7 have no monitoring at all — but the other 7 (WIN-002/012/029/038/045/046/049) do have monitoring domains, all hardcoded to pass.' Use template variable for the total. Even better: make the 7/7 split computed as well.

holes_found.description: WIN-010 incorrectly listed as self-contradicted. The contrast paragraph lists WIN-010 among the hardcoded self-contradicted WINs: 'the other eight (WIN-002/010/012/029/038/045/046/049).' But WIN-010 verdict = 'Std Model Explains', NOT 'Self-Contradicted'. WIN-010 does have monitoring=hardcoded, but it is not self-contradicted. Including it in this list claims a WIN is self-contradicted when our own review says it isn't — a credibility error that a dome defender checking our data against our prose will immediately find. This was flagged in c2 review but NOT fixed by EXP-089.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove WIN-010 from the self-contradicted list. The 7 hardcoded self-contradicted WINs are: WIN-002, 012, 029, 038, 045, 046, 049.

holes_found.description: Standard-physics relabelling count off by one. The stratification says 'Eleven are standard-physics relabellings' but data shows 12 WINs with verdict=Std Model Explains AND monitoring=none (IDs: 020, 025, 035, 039, 040, 043, 059, 060, 063, 064, 066, 068). WIN-068 was miscounted in the self-contradicted bucket; moving it here gives the correct 12.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'Eleven' to 'Twelve' or, better yet, use a template variable. This corrects the knock-on error from the WIN-068 misclassification.

holes_found.description: Stratification paragraph uses hardcoded number words instead of template variables. 'Eight', 'Fifteen', 'Eleven', 'Three', 'Two' are all hardcoded words for counts that should be computed. {{TALLY_MISLEADING}} exists as a template variable (defined in sections.json meta) but is not used here — 'Fifteen' appears as a literal word. ISS-830 was supposed to fix the 'Twelve → {{TALLY_MISLEADING}}' pattern, but the fix appears to have replaced the word rather than inserted the template variable. The entire stratification is a breakdown of {{CA_NONE}} by verdict, so every sub-count should be templated or at minimum derived from data.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace all hardcoded count words in the stratification with template variables. If variables don't exist yet for the sub-breakdowns, either create them in the build pipeline or add a note that these are point-in-time counts subject to change.

holes_found.description: Duplicate style attribute on the h2 element. The summary tag contains: '<h2 style="display:inline;margin:0" style="display:inline;margin:0">'. The style attribute is duplicated. Browsers will ignore the second one, so no visual impact, but it's sloppy HTML that an integrity check should catch.

stronger_arguments.description: The stratification paragraph's strongest untapped argument is the SYSTEMATIC pattern of monitoring gaps. Right now it lists categories and counts but doesn't synthesize the pattern. The dome's monitoring is present (hardcoded) for claims where the value is known and static, and absent for claims where genuine testing would be damaging. This is not random negligence — it's a selection effect. The final sentence ('The gaps are systematic') states this but doesn't prove it. A stronger version would note: self-contradicted WINs have 50% monitoring (all hardcoded), refuted-by-data WINs have 33% monitoring (all hardcoded), but misleading/std-physics WINs have 0% monitoring. The monitoring rate DECREASES as the verdict severity increases.

The c2 review's strongest untapped argument remains: the dome's own internal data sources disagree on accuracy (96.3%, 100%, 89.3%, 94.5% from four endpoints). This is mentioned nowhere in 2b.1. It powerfully compounds the 'accuracy figure is not computed' argument — even if it WERE computed, the dome's own infrastructure can't agree on what it should be.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-089 restructure genuinely improves the section's rhetorical impact. Leading with '58.6% have no validation code at all' is a stronger opening than leading with hardcoded monitoring. The bolded emphasis and the progression from 'most shocking' (none) to 'next most damaging' (hardcoded) to 'even the live ones' (globe-calibrated) creates a proper argumentative cascade. The stratification paragraph is structurally the right move — breaking down the 41 unmonitored WINs by verdict category shows the gaps are systematic, not random.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The factual errors in the stratification and contrast paragraphs (WIN-068 misclassified, WIN-010 incorrectly included, counts wrong by 1-2 in every category) are the exact errors that undermine the restructure's goal. The whole point is to show we've done rigorous per-WIN analysis of the monitoring gaps. Getting specific WIN classifications wrong when our own data disagrees with our own prose is the kind of self-contradiction we critique the dome model for. Four major factual errors in two paragraphs is too many for a section whose premise is meticulous code auditing.

deeper_analysis:
This is a frustrating review because the EXP-089 restructure does exactly the right thing at the macro level (leading with the most impactful finding) while leaving multiple factual errors at the micro level that were already flagged in the c2 review.

The four major holes all stem from the same root cause: the stratification and contrast paragraphs were written (or updated) using stale or incorrect WIN-to-verdict mappings. The section claims 16 self-contradicted WINs when there are 14. It includes WIN-010 (Std Model Explains) and WIN-068 (Std Model Explains) in the self-contradicted category. These aren't judgment calls — they're lookup errors against our own wins.json.

The c2 review flagged the WIN count issues (c2 noted WIN-045/046/049 were hardcoded not unmonitored, and the misleading count was wrong). EXP-089 fixed the monitoring classification issue (the stratification now only lists monitoring=none WINs) and fixed the misleading count (12→15). But it introduced a NEW error: WIN-068 is now listed as self-contradicted when it's Std Model Explains. And the pre-existing errors of '16 self-contradicted WINs' and 'WIN-010 in the hardcoded SC list' were not addressed.

The TLDR mismatch is the most reader-impactful issue. A reader who sees only the TLDR and then skips to another section will take away 'hardcoded monitoring is the problem.' The body says 'the MAJORITY have no monitoring at all.' These aren't contradictory but they have different emphasis, and the whole point of EXP-089 was to fix the emphasis.

Recommended fix priority: (1) Update TLDR to mention none/unmonitored as lead finding. (2) Fix WIN-068 classification (move from SC to std-physics, adjust counts 8→7 and 11→12). (3) Fix total SC count 16→14 and remove WIN-010 from the list. (4) Replace hardcoded count words with template variables where possible.

On the other side: a dome defender reading this section will focus on the '58.6% unmonitored' lead claim. That claim is correct and devastating. The factual errors are all in the DETAIL stratification that most readers won't expand. So the practical risk is lower than 'four major holes' suggests — but any defender who does expand will find a paragraph where we miscategorize our own WINs, which is exactly the kind of sloppy analysis we accuse the dome of.
